data.title: Swiss Producer and Import Prices Fall More Slowly
data.description:
Switzerland’s producer and import prices fell 0.3% year-on-year in January 2025, easing from a 0.9% decline in the previous month.

This marked the 21st consecutive period of falling prices but the mildest drop since the trend began in May 2023.

Producer prices were unchanged after slipping 0.1% in December, while import costs declined 0.8%, moderating from a 2.6% fall.

On a monthly basis, producer and import prices edged up 0.1% after stagnating in the prior period, driven by higher costs for petroleum products, watches, and medical and dental instruments.

Meanwhile, electricity prices declined.

data.title: NatWest Results Look Solid — Market Talk
data.description: NatWest reported a solid set of results and upgraded guidance that aligns with consensus, Keefe, Bruyette & Woods says in a research note after the British lender's quarterly print. It guided for a return on tangible equity between 15% and 16% for 2025, while consensus estimates stood at 15.7%. NatWest also guided for total income for the year in the 15.2 billion to 15.7 billion pound range, in line with expectations of 15.5 billion pounds. The lack of share-buyback announcement might be slightly disappointing, analysts write, noting the 15.5 pence-a-share dividend was better than expected. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

data.title: Segro Returns to Profit in FY24; Revenue Down
data.description:
Segro on Friday reported a return to profit for 2024 while revenue decreased year over year.

Profit after tax for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 594 million pounds sterling, compared with a loss of 253 million pounds earlier. EPS came in at 0.446 pound from a loss per share of 0.207 pound.

The British real estate investment trust's revenue was 675 million pounds, compared with 749 million pounds a year ago.

Analysts polled by FactSet were expecting 664.4 million pounds in sales, 481.6 million pounds in net income, and 0.35 pound in EPS.

Meanwhile, the board recommended a final dividend of 0.202 pound per share for the second half, payable on May 14 to shareholders on record March 28. It paid 0.191 pound per share a year ago. This takes the full-year dividend to 0.293 pound per share, up from 0.278 pound in the previous year.

data.title: Chinese Stocks Closes Week With Gains on Ongoing DeepSeek Popularity; Daan Gene Soars 10%
data.description:
China's shares rebounded at the close of Friday's trade as DeepSeek continues to boost investor sentiment, while the central bank committed to keep the exchange rate stable.

The Shanghai Composite Index added 0.4%, or 14.24 points, to cap the trading week at 3,346.72. The Shenzhen Component Index climbed 1.2%, or 122.83 points, to 10,749.46.

Investors continue to vie for Chinese tech equities after the launch of large language model DeepSeek.

Moreover, the People's Bank of China pledge to keep the exchange rate stable also lifted market sentiment, despite the US' plans to impose reciprocal tariffs.

The reciprocal tariffs will be charged on every country that imposes duties on US goods.

In corporate news, Daan Gene soared 10% as its unit obtained a medical device registration certificate from the Chinese drug administration for an assay kit detecting syphilis treponema.

Hainan Haiyao climbed 3.7% after announcing that the flufenidone capsules developed by a unit were included in the Chinese drug administration's list of breakthrough therapeutic products.

Super Telecom rose 3.2% after appointing Guo Yanqi as chief financial officer to succeed Hu Hongyue.

data.title: NatWest Group's FY24 Profit Rises, Total Income Declines
data.description:
NatWest Group said Friday its profit for the full year 2024 rose, while total income fell year over year.

Profit attributable to ordinary shareholders for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 4.52 billion pounds sterling, compared with 4.39 billion pounds a year ago. EPS moved to 0.531 pound from 0.477 pound.

The British lender's total income was 14.70 billion pounds, compared with 14.75 billion pounds earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet were expecting 14.57 billion pounds in sales, 4.25 billion pounds in net income, and 0.50 pound in EPS.

Meanwhile, the board proposed a final dividend of 0.155 pound per share for the period, payable on April 28 to shareholders on record March 14. It paid 0.115 pound per share a year ago. The final dividend takes the full-year 2024 dividend to 0.215 pound per share, up from 0.17 pound per share in 2023.

For 2025, NatWest Group expects an income between 15.2 billion pounds and 15.7 billion pounds, excluding notable items.

data.title: Birkin Bag Maker Hermes Posts Higher Revenue on Strong Americas Performance
data.description:
By Andrea Figueras

French luxury group Hermes reported higher sales for the final quarter of 2024 after a strong performance in the Americas, adding to signs of a potential rebound in demand for luxury goods.

The maker of the Birkin bag reported on Friday revenue of 3.96 billion euros ($4.15 billion) for the fourth quarter, up nearly 18% at constant exchange rates compared with the prior-year period.

The result compares with analysts' expectations of 3.73 billion euros, according to a Visible Alpha poll of estimates.

For the year as a whole, the group made earnings before interest and taxes of 6.15 billion euros compared with 5.65 billion euros previously. Net profit jumped to 4.60 billion euros, surpassing analysts' prospects of 4.51 billion euros.

The company confirmed its expectations for the medium-term of revenue growth at constant exchange rates, but cautioned on global economic, geopolitical and monetary uncertainties.

Write to Andrea Figueras at andrea.figueras@wsj.com

data.title: Agility Global to Halt Trading on Abu Dhabi Bourse Amid Delay in Financial Results
data.description:
Agility Global (ADX:AGILITY) shares will temporarily pause trading on the Abu Dhabi Securities Exchange from Friday, according to a same-day release.

The trading suspension follows the Abu Dhabi-listed investment and assets management company's failure to file its financial results.

data.title: Yida China Logs 62 Million Yuan in January Contracted Sales
data.description:
Yida China recorded contracted sales of around 62 million yuan in January, with a gross floor area of 4,366 square meters, a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong bourse said.

The property company's average selling price for the month was approximately 14,122 yuan per square meter.

Trading in the company's shares has been suspended since April 2, 2024.

data.title: Romania Q4 GDP Growth Misses Forecast
data.description:
The Romanian GDP expanded by 0.7% year-on-year in the fourth quarter of 2024, slowing from a 1.2% rise in the previous three-month period and missing market expectations of a 1.5% growth, preliminary estimates showed.

This marked the fifteenth consecutive quarter of economic expansion albeit at a softer pace.

On a seasonally adjusted quarterly basis, the country's economy advanced by 0.8% in Q4, accelerating from an upwardly revised 0.1% increase in the previous quarter.

data.title: Singapore's Economy Should Stay Resilient — Market Talk
data.description: Singapore economy is expected to stay resilient this year, as strong local wage and employment growth should help cushion the impact of growing trade protectionism, says RHB Bank's Barnabas Gan. The city-state's negative output gap closed in 4Q 2024, coinciding with stronger-than-expected growth, Gan writes in a note. The shift toward a positive output gap is likely to continue into 1H, he says. Fiscal policy measures are likely to focus on stimulating domestic economic activity, he adds. RHB maintains Singapore's 2025 GDP growth forecast at 3.0%, which is at the top end of the official 1.0%-3.0% range. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: Chinese Funded $546 Million Steel Plant to Start Operations in Bolivia
data.description:
A China-funded steel plant costing $546 million in Bolivia will start production in February with enough capacity to cover half the country's needs, Reuters reported Thursday, citing Bolivian officials.

The Mutun plant, originally planned about 50 years back, was mainly funded by the Export-Import Bank of China and will be operated by China's Sinosteel Engineering and Technology for its first year.

The plant is expected to produce 200,000 metric tons of steel a year, principally rebar and wire mesh, worth $260 million, the publication said.

data.title: Barclays PLC To End Share Buybacks By Nov 13
Hermès Reports Higher FY24 Attributable Net Income, Revenue
data.description:
Hermès International said Friday its net income and revenue for the full year 2024 increased year over year.

Net income attributable to owners of the parent for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 4.60 billion euros, compared with 4.31 billion euros earlier. EPS moved to 43.87 euros from 41.12 euros.

The luxury fashion house's revenue was 15.17 billion euros, compared with 13.43 billion euros a year ago.

Analysts polled by FactSet were expecting 14.93 billion euros in sales, 4.49 billion euros in net income, and 42.86 euros in EPS.

Meanwhile, the board intends to propose an ordinary dividend of 16 euros per share and an exceptional dividend of 10 euros per share for the period. It paid an ordinary dividend of 15 euros per share and an exceptional dividend of 10 euros per share a year ago.

Looking ahead, Hermès affirmed its medium-term guidance of revenue growth at constant exchange rates.

data.title: Wood Group Assessing Refinancing Options; Says 4Q Was Weak
data.description:
(07:00 GMT) *Wood Group Sees FY24 Adjusted Ebitda of $450M-$460M
(07:01 GMT) *Wood Group Sees FY24 Adjusted EBIT of $205M-$215M
(07:01 GMT) *Wood Group Taking Actions to Mitigate Weaker-Than-Expected Trading in 4Q
(07:02 GMT) *Wood Group Cancels Executive, Employee Bonuses
(07:02 GMT) *Wood Group Actively Managing Working Cap at Yr End
(07:02 GMT) *Wood Group Order Book Increased to $6.2B at Dec 31
(07:04 GMT) *Wood Group Continues to Expect Good Growth in 2025 Supported by Cost Actions
(07:05 GMT) *Wood Group Expects Double-Digit Adjusted Ebitda, Adjusted EBIT Growth in 2025
(07:05 GMT) *Wood Group Expects Negative Free Cash Flow of $150M-$200M in 2025
(07:05 GMT) *Wood Group Average Net Debt in 2025 Seen in Line With 2024 Levels
(07:06 GMT) *Wood Group Average Net Debt in 2025 Seen Around $1.1B
(07:11 GMT) *Wood Group Assessing All Potential Refinancing Options
(07:11 GMT) *Wood Group Engaging With Lenders on Options
(07:32 GMT) Wood Group Assessing Refinancing Options; Says 4Q Was Weak

By Ian Walker

John Wood Group said it is reviewing all financing options and is in talks with its lenders as most of its debt facilities are due to end in October, and that it had a weaker-than-expected fourth quarter.

The FTSE 250-listed energy-services company said Friday that it expects to report adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization for the year ended Dec. 31 of between $450 million and $460 million and adjusted EBIT of $205 million to $215 million.

The board has taken actions to mitigate the weaker-than-expected fourth quarter, including cancelling executive and employee bonuses and managing working capital at year end, it said.

Due to these actions, the board expects double-digit adjusted Ebitda and adjusted EBIT growth for the year ahead, but negative free cash flow of $150 million to $200 million.

Write to Ian Walker at ian.walker@wsj.com

data.title: Taiwan Could Weather Potential U.S. Tariffs — Market Talk
data.description: Taiwan is a potential target for reciprocal and semiconductor levies as Trump's tariff threats escalate, DBS's Ma Tieying says in a note. The senior economist estimates a hit of up to 0.9 percentage point to the island's GDP growth if such tariffs are imposed. However, Taiwan could mitigate the impact through bilateral negotiations, such as by reducing its import tariff rates on American goods, increasing imports of U.S. agricultural products, energy and automobiles, as well as boosting semiconductor investments in the U.S., she says. "A fiscal policy response is more likely, with targeted support for industries and companies negatively impacted by tariffs." DBS maintains its forecast for Taiwan's economy to grow 3.0% this year. (farah.elias@wsj.com)

data.title: Chinese Shares End Higher, Supported by Pharma, Software Stocks — Market Talk
data.description: Chinese shares ended higher as pharmaceutical and software stocks benefitted from the AI momentum. Shenzhen Mindray Bio-Medical Electronics rose 4.3% and WuXi AppTec gained 4.6%. Nomura analyst Jialin Zhang believes that investors are betting on the sector's AI potential, especially in areas like diagnosis, contract research and drug development, following the recent DeepSeek breakthrough. Amid the software makers, iFlytek gained 3.4% and Shanghai Baosight Software rose 8.0%. Property stocks fell broadly, with Poly Developments & Holdings Group down 0.8% and China Vanke dropping 2.0%. The benchmark Shanghai Composite Index ended 0.4% higher at 3346.72, the Shenzhen Composite Index rose 0.75% and the ChiNext Price Index advanced 1.8%. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

data.title: Hazoor Multi Projects' Consolidated Profit Slumps in Fiscal Q3; Shares Drop 11%
data.description:
Hazoor Multi Projects saw a sharp decline in its consolidated profit to 27.1 million Indian rupees in the fiscal third quarter ended Dec. 31, 2024, against 98.3 million rupees a year ago.

Earnings per share contracted to 0.137 rupees compared with the year-ago figure of 0.745 rupees, according to a Thursday filing to the BSE by the infrastructure company.

Revenue from operations in fiscal Q3 jumped to 1.65 billion rupees from 806.3 million rupees a year earlier.

Shares of the company slumped over 11% in recent trade.

data.title: Sudarshan Chemical Industries Forms New Step-Down Subsidiary in Dubai
data.description:
Sudarshan Chemical Industries has incoporated a step-down subsidiary in Dubai, UAE, named Sudarshan Middle East General Trading L.L.C, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

Sudarshan Middle East is wholly owned by Sudarshan Europe B.V., which is a wholly-owned subsidiary of the Indian company.

The newly incorporated company will mainly be in same line of business of pigments manufacturing and supply as that of the parent company.

data.title: What are the main events for today?
data.description:
In the European session we don't have much on the agenda other than a couple of low tier releases. As it's usually the case, most of the action will be in the American session when we get the US Retail Sales, the US import prices and the US Industrial Production and Capacity Utilization data.

13:30 GMT/08:30 ET - US January Retail Sales

The US Retail Sales M/M is expected at -0.1% vs. 0.4% prior, while the ex-Autos figure is seen at 0.3% vs. 0.4% prior. The focus will be on the Control Group figure which is expected at 0.3% vs. 0.7% prior.

Consumer spending has been stable which is something you would expect given the positive real wage growth and resilient labour market. More recently, we’ve been seeing some easing in consumer sentiment though which might also lead to some softening in consumer spending.

If the data indeed softens, it shouldn’t be worrying just yet but should help alleviate further some inflation worries and keep the market pricing around two rate cuts in 2025.

US Retail Sales YoY

Central bank speakers:

  • 20:00 GMT/15:00 ET - Fed's Logan (neutral - non voter)

(07:07 GMT) *NatWest 2024 Total Dividend 21.5p
(07:14 GMT) *NatWest Sees 2025 RoTE 15%-16%
(07:34 GMT) NatWest Guides For Higher Income, Profitability After Quarterly Beat

By Elena Vardon

NatWest said it expects an acceleration in income growth and profitability this year after it posted fourth-quarter results that beat expectations.

The British bank forecasts that its total income excluding notable items will come in between 15.2 billion pounds and 15.7 billion pounds ($19.10 billion-$19.73 billion) for 2025 as loan growth and structural hedges that continue to mitigate the impact of interest-rate cuts by the Bank of England. This is up from the 14.70 billion pound result it reported for 2024, which was roughly flat on the previous year.

It also guided for its return on tangible equity--a key measure of profitability--to land between 15% and 16% this year, and expects the metric to be greater than 15% in 2027. This compares with a previous target of a return above 13% for 2026 and a 17.5% result last year.

The lender--which the U.K. government seeks to return to private ownership in the first half of this year--posted a 19% on-year increase in its pretax profit of 1.49 billion pounds for the three months ended Dec. 31, beating estimates of 1.36 billion pounds taken from a company-compiled consensus.

Fourth-quarter total income rose 8% to 3.825 billion pounds, ahead of the 3.71 billion pounds forecast by analysts.

NatWest's common equity Tier 1 ratio--a key measure of balance-sheet strength--stood at 13.6% at Dec. 31, in line with expectations and its mid-term target of between 13% and 14%.

Write to Elena Vardon at elena.vardon@wsj.com

data.title: Robinsons Retail Holdings Target Price Raised to PHP57.50 From PHP50.22 by SB Equities >RRHI.PH
Coca-Cola Europacific Partners Posts Higher FY24 Attributable Profit, Revenue
data.description:
Coca-Cola Europacific Partners said Friday profit and revenue for 2024 rose year over year.

Profit attributable to shareholders for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 1.42 billion euros, compared with 1.67 billion euros earlier. EPS moved to 3.08 euros from 3.63 euros.

The bottling company's revenue was 20.44 billion euros, compared with 18.30 billion euros a year ago.

Coca-Cola Europacific Partners maintained its 2025 guidance in line with its mid-term strategic objectives with revenue expected to rise 4% and operating profit expected to increase by 7%.

data.title: Nordic Aqua Resumes Salmon Sales at Chinese Facility
data.description:
Nordic Aqua Partners said Thursday it restarted Atlantic salmon sales at its Ningbo facility in China after addressing operational challenges.

The land-based salmon farming company has taken measures to counteract geosmin and implemented enhanced operational protocols, according to a release.

Nordic Aqua added that construction for the second-stage expansion of the facility is also underway, with a first harvest expected in the third quarter of 2026.

data.title: KOSPI Extend Gains for 4th Day
data.description:
The benchmark KOSPI rose 0.3% to close at 2,591 points on Friday, extending gains for the fourth straight session and hitting its highest level since late October 2024.

For the week, the benchmark index also advanced 2.7%, its biggest weekly gain since early January.

Markets appeared relieved after US President Donald Trump signed a memo on reciprocal tariffs, signaling no immediate tariff imposition.

However, the US continues to investigate unfair trade practices, with South Korea, among its key targets, adding to market uncertainty.

In response, South Korea's acting President Choi Sang-mok stated that the government would review non-tariff barriers and vulnerabilities to address the plans.

In economic news, the jobless rate in South Korea fell to 2.9% in January, down from December’s three-year high of 3.7%.

Among key stocks, SK Hynix (0.7%), Celltrion (1.6%), Hanwha Aerospace (3.3%), and Hyundai Mobis (2.7%) were the top performers.

data.title: Segro Swings to Pretax Profit, Rental Income Rises
data.description:
(07:01 GMT) *Segro 2024 New Headline Rent GBP91M
(07:02 GMT) *Segro 2024 Pre-Items, Pre-Tax Pft GBP470M Vs. Pft GBP409M
(07:03 GMT) *Segro 2024 Net Rental Income GBP628M
(07:03 GMT) *Segro Analysts Saw Net Rental Income at GBP578.6M
(07:04 GMT) *Segro 2024 Pretax Pft GBP636M Vs. Loss GBP263M
(07:05 GMT) *Segro Declares Dividend of 29.3p
(07:07 GMT) *Segro 2024 Portfolio Value Rose 1.1%
(07:29 GMT) Segro Swings to Pretax Profit, Rental Income Rises

By Anthony O. Goriainoff

Segro said it swung to a pretax profit as rental income increased, and that the business was primed for further growth.

The U.K. real-estate investment trust said on Friday that 2024 pretax profit was 636 million pounds ($799.2 million) compared with a pretax loss of 263 million pounds the year before.

Adjusted pretax profit--the company's preferred metric, which strips out exceptional and other one-off items--was 470 million pounds compared with 409 million pounds.

Headline rent rose to 91 million pounds from 88 million pounds the year prior.

Net rental income rose to 628 million pounds from 587 million pounds, driven by strong like-for-like rental growth of 5.8% and development completions. Consensus for the metric was 578.6 million pounds, taken from FactSet and based on three analysts' forecast.

Adjusted net asset value--a key metric for the industry--stood at 907 pence a share at Dec. 31, flat on the year prior, reflecting a 1.1% like-for-like portfolio value rise versus a 4% decline the year before.

The board declared a final dividend of 20.2 pence a share, up from 19.1 pence the year prior, taking the full year dividend to 29.3 pence, a 5.4% rise.

"Momentum has carried into the early weeks of 2025, and we take confidence from the positive conversations we are having with our customers across all of our markets.," Chief Executive David Sleath said.

Write to Anthony O. Goriainoff at anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com
